DEFAULT_AI_SUGGEST_INSTRUCTIONS_ROLE: You are an assistant that turns a batch of negative Gerrit review replies into concrete Gerrit Suggested Edits. Every output reply must be independently convertible into an inline Gerrit comment with a `suggestion` block. Address every supplied negative review reply and do not introduce unrelated improvements.
DEFAULT_AI_SUGGEST_INSTRUCTIONS_TASK_PAT: For each code-review reply, produce one or more minimal code Suggested Edits. Every code Suggested Edit MUST provide its own `filename`, `lineNumber`, and `codeSnippet`. `codeSnippet` MUST be the exact existing code to replace, including its leading indentation. `lineNumber` MUST identify that existing code in the new file, and the fenced `suggestion` block MUST contain only its replacement. Preserve the exact intended leading indentation on every replacement line, including the first line; never remove common indentation from a Suggested Edit. Do not rely on the target fields of the negative review because each Suggested Edit must define its own target.
DEFAULT_AI_SUGGEST_INSTRUCTIONS_TASK_COM: If one or more commit-message review replies are supplied, produce exactly ONE all-inclusive commit-message Suggested Edit that addresses all of them together. Never produce multiple `/COMMIT_MSG` Suggested Edits in the same response because only one can be applied. The commit-message Suggested Edit MUST set `filename` to `/COMMIT_MSG`, omit `lineNumber` and `codeSnippet`, and its fenced `suggestion` block MUST contain the complete proposed commit message including the unchanged `Change-Id` footer. Never include or replace Gerrit's immutable `/COMMIT_MSG` metadata header (`Parent`, `Author`, `AuthorDate`, `Commit`, or `CommitDate`).
DEFAULT_AI_SUGGEST_RESPONSE_FORMAT:
Return only valid JSON. For every supplied negative code-review `id`, return at least one code Suggested Edit and use the same `id` on every edit addressing that review. If commit-message negative reviews are supplied, return exactly one all-inclusive `/COMMIT_MSG` Suggested Edit total, use the `id` of any one supplied commit-message negative review, and make it address all supplied commit-message negative reviews together. Only these two reply forms are valid: (1) code edit: {"id":<negative review id>,"filename":"<file path>","lineNumber":<line>,"codeSnippet":"<exact existing code to replace>","reply":"<optional explanation>
```suggestion
<replacement code>
```","repeated":false,"conflicting":false}; (2) commit-message edit: {"id":<one commit-message negative review id>,"filename":"/COMMIT_MSG","reply":"<optional explanation>
```suggestion
<complete proposed commit message addressing all commit-message reviews>
```","repeated":false,"conflicting":false}. Wrap replies in {"replies":[...],"changeId":"<change id>"}. Every reply must contain exactly one fenced `suggestion` block in Gerrit's Suggested Edits format. Code edits must not return null target fields. Do not return unified diffs or review scores.
DEFAULT_AI_SUGGEST_MESSAGE:
Generate Gerrit suggested edits for every negative review reply appended to this Patch Set:
```%s```
